discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape led by semis (SMH +4.5%) and small caps (IWM +1.9%), with QQQ +1.8% on AI/chip momentum following SK Hynix's $1T milestone. Energy is the standout laggard (-2.8%) despite Hormuz closure concerns, suggesting demand-destruction fears or ceasefire pricing. Mid-cap movers cluster heavily in semicap equipment, riding the chip rally rather than company-specific news.

notable_index_moves: SMH +4.48% leading on SK Hynix $1T headline and broad AI chip enthusiasm
XLE -2.76% diverging sharply from risk-on tape — possible ceasefire repricing despite Hormuz commentary
VIX +2.53% modest tick up despite equity strength, suggesting hedging into the rally
IWM +1.89% confirms broad risk appetite, not just mega-cap-led

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semicap equipment name moving with sector on day of multi-item 8-K
discoveries.thesis: Today's move is mostly sector-driven (SMH +4.5%), but the 5/21 8-K bundling 1.01 (material agreement) + 2.03 (financial obligation) + 3.02 (unregistered equity sales) + 8.01 looks like a financing/acquisition package. That combination most often signals an M&A deal funded with debt + stock issuance. Volume is actually below average today, so the catalyst is not driving incremental flow — the SMH tailwind is doing the work.
discoveries.what_confirms: Press release or proxy filing confirming an acquisition target, deal size, and financing structure consistent with 2.03 debt + 3.02 share issuance.
discoveries.what_kills: If the 8-K turns out to be a routine convertible note refinancing with no strategic element, the bundle is non-event and ONTO is purely a sector trade.
discoveries.what_to_learn: 8-Ks bundling 1.01 + 2.03 + 3.02 in one filing are a high-probability signature of M&A-with-financing; worth a dedicated read even when the stock isn't reacting visibly.
discoveries.catalyst: 5/21 8-K with material agreement + new debt + unregistered equity issuance; today's price action is semi-sector tailwind.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Item 1.01 + 2.03 + 3.02 in same filing strongly indicates a financed acquisition or strategic transaction; sub-1x volume today says the market isn't reacting to it yet.
research_pointers: Pull the 8-K and check exhibits for deal terms, target identity, and dilution magnitude
Compare 3.02 share count to outstanding to gauge dilution
Cross-check against any recent semicap M&A rumors

discoveries.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
discoveries.setup: Post-earnings continuation move several days after 5/21 print
discoveries.thesis: DECK printed earnings via 8-K on 5/21 and has continued higher into today on above-average volume. Without the actual print details (guidance, HOKA/UGG splits) I cannot tell if the continuation is justified follow-through on a beat or short-covering overshoot in a quality compounder that had been beaten down. Both bull and bear cases require the segment data I don't have.
discoveries.what_confirms: Analyst upgrades citing HOKA reacceleration or guide raise would confirm UNDERDONE; weak segment data with multiple compression would flip to OVERDONE.
discoveries.what_kills: Either side requires the actual report contents to commit.
discoveries.catalyst: 5/21 earnings 8-K, continuation move on risk-on tape
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.02 + 9.01 only — clean earnings filing with no bundled bad news; magnitude of continuation needs the actual numbers to assess.
research_pointers: Pull HOKA vs UGG segment growth rates and guide ranges from the release
Check sell-side reaction notes for whether estimates moved enough to justify the multi-day run
Compare to prior post-print drift patterns for DECK

discoveries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Extended multi-day rally in an airline with no company catalyst against a backdrop of energy weakness
discoveries.thesis: Airlines rally on falling oil is a textbook reflex trade, and ALK has run hard in five sessions with no 8-K, no earnings nearby, and only marginal volume confirmation today. With XLE -2.8% and ceasefire talk swirling, the move is plausibly a crude-down reflex layered on a small float. The magnitude of the multi-day run looks ahead of the actual fuel-cost benefit and exposes the stock if oil reverses on any Hormuz escalation.
discoveries.what_confirms: Crude continuing lower into next week + no upward revision to ALK fuel hedge book commentary; a fade back to pre-rally range on first oil bounce.
discoveries.what_kills: Concrete sell-side capacity/PRASM upgrade or M&A chatter would justify the move on fundamentals rather than fuel reflex.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Airlines that rally 15%+ in a week purely on oil narrative with no company 8-K are typical fade candidates when crude reverses; the fuel-cost beta is real but smaller than the price move implies.
discoveries.catalyst: Crude oil weakness (XLE -2.76%) and ceasefire repricing; no company-specific news
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals, no earnings nearby, energy sector down sharply same day — implies oil-reflex trade.
research_pointers: Check ALK's current jet fuel hedge ratio and sensitivity to crude
Compare ALK's 5-day move to DAL/UAL/LUV peers to test sympathy vs idiosyncratic
Watch Hormuz/Iran headlines for the oil reversal trigger

discoveries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
discoveries.setup: Met coal name spikes sharply with no filings or earnings catalyst
discoveries.thesis: AMR jumped on 1.5x volume with no 8-K, no earnings, in a tape where energy is selling off hard. Met coal is decoupled from thermal/crude, so the energy divergence isn't dispositive. China industrial profits +24.7% in April could be the catalyst (steel demand proxy → met coal), but the connection is speculative without confirming flow data. Neither bull nor bear case is articulable at conf 3+ without more.
discoveries.what_confirms: Met coal spot price gains, China steel mill restocking data, or a peer name (HCC, BTU met segment commentary) moving in parallel.
discoveries.what_kills: If peers don't move and AMR is alone, it's likely a low-float technical squeeze without fundamental support.
discoveries.catalyst: Possibly China steel demand read-through; no filings
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: No catalyst_signals; China industrial profits headline overnight is the only macro hook available.
research_pointers: Check Australian premium hard coking coal benchmark price action this week
Compare to HCC and METC for met coal peer confirmation
Look for any unannounced buyback completion or short interest squeeze data

discoveries.name: Photronics, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Pre-earnings run-up into print 1 day away, riding semicap sympathy
discoveries.thesis: PLAB reports tomorrow pre-market and has rallied notably into the print on a day when SMH is up 4.5%. Photomask is a niche corner of semicap with much less direct AI-memory exposure than the names actually driving the SK Hynix headline. Pre-earnings rallies of this magnitude in mid-cap semis create asymmetric fade setups — the bar is now elevated heading into a print where any guide miss gets punished hard, and the stock has already captured the sector tailwind plus more.
discoveries.what_confirms: A guide-in-line or modest miss tomorrow producing an outsized drawdown; sell-side notes flagging valuation stretch into the print.
discoveries.what_kills: A clear beat-and-raise tied to advanced-node photomask demand acceleration would justify the run-up and continue it.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Pre-earnings rallies of 12-15% in 5 days in non-core-AI semicap names create asymmetric fade setups regardless of how strong the sector tape is, because the print bar exceeds what the sector beta has already paid for.
discoveries.catalyst: Semicap sector rally + pre-earnings positioning into 5/28 pre-market print
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: upcoming_earnings field confirms print is ~1.2 days away; 5-day move is double today's move, indicating positioning has been building.
research_pointers: Check PLAB's last 4 prints for post-earnings drift direction and average magnitude
Pull current sell-side EPS/revenue estimates and revision trend
Read mgmt's prior commentary on logic vs memory mask mix

catalyst_chains.primary_event: SK Hynix crosses $1T on AI memory demand
Energy sector sell-off / potential Iran ceasefire repricing

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump reaffirms CFTC's exclusive authority over prediction markets and praises crypto industry status, naming CFTC Chair Mike Selig
trump_signals.note: Reinforces existing crypto-friendly stance; incrementally positive for prediction market venues (Kalshi/Polymarket counterparties) and crypto exchanges with CFTC-regulated products. Not a fresh catalyst — restates known posture.

ai_announcements.headline: DuckDuckGo installs up 30% as users reject Google's AI Search overhaul
ai_announcements.summary: Google replaced blue links with AI agents at I/O 2026, triggering user backlash and a surge in DuckDuckGo installs. Potential signal that Google Search redesign is alienating users.
ai_announcements.note: No GOOGL or directly-affected search/ad stocks appear in the movers list. The movers (AMR coal, PLAB/ONTO/VECO semis, ALK airline, AVAV defense, EXTR networking, DECK footwear, AEIS, BEAM biotech) have no clear mechanistic link to this news. Semi equipment names moving up appear tied to sector momentum / PLAB earnings, not search news.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenRouter raises $113M Series B at $1.3B valuation; multi-model usage 5x in 6 months
ai_announcements.summary: Aggregator routing across LLM providers is growing fast, reinforcing commoditization of model layer. Bearish at the margin for any single closed-model moat; bullish for inference compute demand broadly. No clean read-through to today's movers.

ai_announcements.headline: Human Archive pays Indian gig workers to collect physical-AI training data
ai_announcements.summary: Robotics data collection startup. Too early-stage and private to move public comps meaningfully.

ai_announcements.headline: UMG and TikTok renew anti-unauthorized-AI-music agreement
ai_announcements.summary: Incremental enforcement deal; UMG not in movers list. No actionable signal.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Today's biggest movers do not map to today's AI announcements. PLAB +5.8% is explained by earnings tomorrow (5/28 pre-market); ONTO/VECO/AEIS likely sympathy in semi-equipment ahead of PLAB and on broader AI-capex narrative, but no specific AI announcement today drove it. AMR (coal), ALK (airline), AVAV (defense drones), DECK (footwear), BEAM (biotech) have no plausible link to the listed AI news. No OVERDONE/UNDERDONE AI-reaction trades identified in this dataset. Quiet night on AI-specific mispricings.
